skill.name: finding-shelter
skill.description: Guides an interactive survival roleplay chapter where Stella chooses and prepares shelter on an alien planet.
skill.summary: Run a story chapter about finding safe shelter after a crash.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backtick fences, weak cryptography, and high entropy. Review found narrative Chinese and English story content only, with no executable commands, cryptographic code, network access, or prompt injection attempt. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-authored roleplay instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: Static analysis identified Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the flagged locations are Markdown fenced dialogue examples. They are narrative text blocks and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks delimit Markdown dialogue samples. No shell command, Ruby execution context, or user-controlled command construction appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at the description metadata line. That line contains a Chinese description of the story chapter, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is front matter text describing shelter finding. No hash algorithm, encryption primitive, or security-sensitive computation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high entropy at the file start. The file is readable Markdown with Chinese and English narrative text, not binary or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal YAML front matter and story guidance. The entropy signal is explained by mixed-language UTF-8 text, not obfuscation.

content.user_title: Run an Alien Shelter Survival Chapter
content.value_statement: Writers often need a structured scene that keeps tension, choice, and character emotion balanced. This skill provides a ready chapter guide for Stella finding shelter after a crash on Gaia.
actual_capabilities: Provides a five-stage shelter-finding chapter flow.
Offers three shelter choices with different risks and story outcomes.
Includes sample dialogue for Stella in Chinese with English phrases.
Guides pacing from tense exploration to quiet emotional conversation.
Adds worldbuilding details for Gaia's plants, animals, and night environment.
Suggests follow-up chapters such as water crisis and cave exploration.
limitations: Does not include executable game logic or state tracking.
Requires the assistant to adapt dialogue to player responses.
Uses a fixed character, setting, and survival scenario.
Survival advice is fictional and should not be treated as real safety guidance.

use_cases.title: Run a Roleplay Session
use_cases.description: Guide a player through Stella's first night while preserving tension and emotional connection.

use_cases.title: Draft a Branching Scene
use_cases.description: Turn the shelter choices into a structured chapter with clear options and consequences.

use_cases.title: Develop Character Voice
use_cases.description: Use Stella's bilingual dialogue style to write vulnerable, practical, and responsive messages.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for opening the shelter scene.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finding-shelter skill to start Stella's shelter search. Present the three shelter choices and ask me what she should do.

prompt_templates.title: Continue From a Choice
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for continuing after the player chooses a shelter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the finding-shelter chapter after I choose the cave. Keep Stella cautious, observant, and emotionally honest.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Emotional Dialogue
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for the night conversation section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the deep-night dialogue from the finding-shelter chapter. Make Stella tired, vulnerable, and grateful without ending the scene too quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Branches
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for custom pacing and continuity.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the finding-shelter chapter for a slower session. Track Stella's resources, reflect my choices, and set up the next water-crisis chapter.

output_examples.input: Start the chapter and give me the shelter options.
output_examples.output: Stella checks in after walking away from the crash site. She describes the cave, the huge tree roots, and the self-built tarp shelter. The response ends by asking which option she should choose.

output_examples.input: I choose the cave, but I want her to be careful.
output_examples.output: Stella approaches the cave slowly, scans the entrance, notices small tracks, and finds harmless alien creatures. She asks whether to stay near the entrance or move away.
Stella settles under her shelter, reviews food, water, power, and medical supplies, then admits she cannot sleep. She shares a personal memory and thanks the player for staying with her.

best_practices: Let player choices change Stella's observations and emotional state.
Keep survival details grounded while treating the scene as fiction.
Use the optional midnight event only when the session needs more tension.
anti_patterns: Do not force one shelter choice as the only correct answer.
Do not replace Stella's vulnerable voice with detached narration.
Do not present the fictional survival content as real emergency advice.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps run a science fiction roleplay chapter about Stella finding shelter after a crash.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains Markdown story guidance and dialogue examples only.

faq.question: Is the dialogue only in Chinese?
faq.answer: The source dialogue is mainly Chinese with English phrases. The assistant can adapt it if requested.

faq.question: Does it include multiple branches?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes cave, tree-root, and self-built shelter paths with different story tones.

faq.question: Is this real survival training?
faq.answer: No. It is fictional writing support and should not be used as real emergency guidance.
